changes_made: Changed k8s_configmap_previous_versions_retained to k8s_config_previous_versions_retained for consistency with resource naming conventions
review_notes: The control CM-2 (3) is applicable to Kubernetes as it involves retaining previous configurations, which can be mapped to Kubernetes resources like ConfigMaps and etcd backups. The function names mostly follow the naming conventions, but a minor change was needed for consistency. The functions are technically accurate and map well to the control requirements.
naming_improvements: Use 'config' instead of 'configmap' for resource name to align with naming standards
title: Baseline Configuration | Retention of Previous Configurations
step1_reasoning: The control CM-2 (3) focuses on retaining previous configurations, which can be mapped to Kubernetes by ensuring that configuration data (such as ConfigMaps) has a mechanism for retaining previous versions. Additionally, etcd backups are crucial for retaining previous states of the cluster configuration. Audit log retention ensures that changes to configurations can be traced over time.
